steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Add 29r to both sides: q = s + 29r.

choiceAnalysis:
• '$q = s - 29r$': Subtracting 29r again doubles the subtraction; incorrect. ✗
  • '$q = s + 29r$': Adding 29r to both sides of q - 29r = s gives q = s + 29r. ✓
  • '$q = 29rs$': Multiplies terms that were added/subtracted; wrong operation entirely. ✗
  • '$q = -\frac{s}{29r}$': Involves division and negation with no algebraic basis from the original equation. ✗
That matches the correct answer: B is correct.
